articles.title: Global air passenger demand falls 3.4% amid West Asia crisis: IATA
articles.description: Global air passenger demand dropped by 3.4% in April 2026, dragged down by a 46.6% fall for Middle East carriers due to regional tensions, reports IATA. Excluding the Middle East, demand saw a modest increase of 1.2% amid volatile conditions.

articles.title: India needs 216 multimodal logistics parks by 2047 for smooth freight movement: CII
articles.description: A Union government official had told HT that the government had now downscaled its approach to build only nine of them, primarily due to land acquisition constraints

articles.title: Nifty earnings growth slashed to 8.5% from 14%; no comfort in valuations still: Amish Shah
articles.description: BofA Securities warns Indian equities face rich valuations and halved earnings growth outlook, projecting market gains to mirror profit expansion. Elevated valuations over emerging market peers and domestic bonds suggest limited upside, with returns likely in the 8-9% range. Steady domestic investor flows are expected to prevent sharp corrections, favoring a sideways market movement.

articles.title: Man claims hotel asked him to pay ₹8,000 for broken mirror, shares travel tip that 'can save thousands'
articles.description: An X user’s hotel stay experience has gone viral after he claimed he was billed ₹8,000 for a mirror he says he did not damage.

articles.title: Live: Tech stocks buck subdued market mood; VIX spikes | Closing Bell
articles.description: Indian markets ended lower for the third straight session, with the Sensex slipping over 200 points from the day’s high amid weak market breadth and rising volatility. The India VIX jumped more than 4%, reflecting cautious investor sentiment. While the midcap index snapped its four-day winning streak, the smallcap index extended gains for the fourth consecutive day. The IT sector emerged as the top performer, with Infosys, Tech Mahindra and HCL Tech leading gains on the Nifty 50, while Indigo, Eicher Motors and Power Grid were among the top losers.

articles.title: Woman compares hiring to speed dating after company revokes offer letter 48 hours before joining
articles.description: A job seeker, Muskan Chauhan, faced a revoked offer letter just two days before her scheduled start date. She had completed multiple interview rounds and a case study. The company cited the original employee's decision to stay. Chauhan highlighted inconsistencies in hiring processes, where candidates face strict expectations while companies can withdraw offers unexpectedly.

articles.title: Plastic currency: RBI revives idea of polymer notes, says report
articles.description: Virendra Pandit New Delhi: India may soon get plastic currency notes as the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has revived a decade-old plan, the media reported on Friday. Asda, majority owned by private equity firm TDR Capital, is Britain's third-largest grocer

articles.description: FairPrice Group first froze prices on 100 everyday essentials in early April, before widening the effort to include more than 300 products and extending the price freeze to May 31.

articles.title: ​Anthropic overtakes OpenAI: AI valuation race enters hyperdrive
articles.description: Anthropic has reportedly overtaken OpenAI in valuation after raising $65 billion in fresh funding, highlighting the rapid rise of enterprise-focused AI platforms. Strong adoption of Claude AI tools, surging revenue growth and aggressive investment in computing infrastructure have intensified competition across the global artificial intelligence industry.

articles.description: Equity mutual funds saw strong performance in May, with returns reaching up to 17%. International funds led the gains, driven by markets like the US, Korea, and Taiwan. While global diversification is beneficial, experts advise caution due to high valuations. Investors should consider a systematic investment plan for global exposure, focusing on long-term data for market assessment.

articles.title: US Stock Market: US growth revised lower; inflation remains elevated
articles.description: The US first-quarter GDP growth was revised down to 1.6%, indicating a slowdown driven by weaker consumer spending and inventory investment. Meanwhile, April's inflation remained elevated, with the PCE price index rising 3.8% year-on-year.

articles.title: US Stock Market: Hawkish Fed voices grow louder amid energy-driven inflation surge
articles.description: A growing split is seen among US Federal Reserve officials. Some policymakers warn that interest rates might need to increase again. This comes as inflation shows signs of not cooling down quickly enough. Rising energy prices linked to the Iran conflict are a major concern. The impact of artificial intelligence on productivity is also being debated.

articles.title: Now, travel in AC bus from Panipat to Delhi border in ₹115

articles.title: 'What's wrong with it?': Bessent backs Trump's face on $250 bill, torches 'terrible' WaPo report
articles.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent revealed that the Treasury Department has already prepared a design for a proposed $250 bill featuring President Donald Trump, should Congress approve legislation authorising the new denomination. Speaking at the White House, Bessent said the final decision rests with lawmakers on Capitol Hill, but stressed that the Treasury has to be prepared if the proposal moves forward. He also pushed back against criticism of the idea, arguing there is nothing inappropriate about Trump’s image being associated with celebrations surrounding America’s 250th anniversary.
